title: Python runtime projection inventory
description: Inventory proving whether generated Python runtime projection files are rendered by command-generation or remain transitional generated-output debt.

kind.description: Stable document kind for the Python runtime projection inventory.

schema_version.description: Schema version for this inventory document.

purpose.description: Human-readable purpose for tracking generated Python runtime projection provenance.

completion_rule.description: Rule that determines whether runtime projection provenance can support full Python generated CLI completion.

allowed_provenance_statuses.description: Allowed provenance status values for runtime projection entries.

completion_rule.description: Rule for accepting exact package-domain runtime source symbols under full Python generated CLI completion.

required_granularity.description: Whole-file package runtime or generated facade acceptance is not sufficient for full Python completion.

status.description: Whether exact symbol-level proof is still required or satisfied.

baseline_symbols.description: Exact accepted runtime boundary symbol ids captured before the #1079 reduction slices so reports can show added and removed accepted runtime symbols.

accepted_runtime_boundaries.description: Exact package-domain runtime boundaries. Whole-file runtime source or generated facade entries are not accepted.

kind.description: Stable document kind for the ordinary-command migration proof section.

status.description: Whether the inventory includes at least one ordinary command migration representative.

rule.description: Rule for proving migrated ordinary command behavior is IR/codegen-owned and fail-closed.

representative_migrations.description: Representative ordinary command migrations that prove the IR/codegen ownership boundary.

ordinary_command_migration.description: Representative ordinary command behavior that has moved behind command-package IR/codegen, plus the remaining runtime primitive boundary.

entries.description: Runtime projection files that must be accounted for before Python generated CLI completion can be claimed.

entry.description: One generated Python runtime projection file and its provenance classification.

path.description: Repo-relative generated Python runtime projection path.

package_id.description: Command package that owns this generated projection.

program.description: CLI program exposed by this generated projection.

artifact_kind.description: Kind of generated Python runtime projection artifact.

provenance_status.description: Whether command-generation renders this file or the file remains transitional generated-output debt.

current_owner.description: Current owner or mechanism responsible for producing this projection.

source_of_truth.description: Contract, IR, or generator surface that acts as the source of truth for this projection.

blocking_full_completion.description: Whether this entry blocks full Python generated CLI completion.

why_blocking.description: Explanation of why the entry blocks or does not block full completion.

required_resolution.description: Resolution required if the entry blocks completion or maintenance action required if it does not.

id.description: Stable identifier for this representative ordinary command migration.

operation_id.description: Generated operation id that owns the migrated command behavior.

adapter_id.description: Command package IR adapter id that binds the command surface to the operation.

source_ir_path.description: Repo-relative command package IR source path.

generated_command_module.description: Repo-relative generated Python command module rendered from the command package IR.

generated_operation_path.description: Repo-relative generated operation contract path reached by the command adapter.

migrated_behavior_fields.description: Command behavior fields that are now owned by command package IR and generated artifacts.

source_module.description: Runtime source module that still owns nondeterministic or mutation behavior.

source_symbol.description: Exact runtime source symbol accepted as the remaining boundary.

runtime_boundary_class.description: Accepted runtime boundary class for the remaining source symbol.

owner.description: Owner responsible for the remaining runtime boundary.

why_runtime_owned.description: Reason this behavior remains runtime-owned instead of deterministic IR/codegen data.

remaining_runtime_boundary.description: Exact accepted runtime boundary that remains after the representative command migration.

fail_closed_rule.description: Rule enforced by checks when the IR, generated command, generated operation, or runtime boundary drifts.

ordinary_command_migration.description: One representative ordinary command migration from handwritten behavior to IR/codegen ownership.

accepted_runtime_boundary.description: One exact accepted package-domain runtime source symbol reached through a generated facade wrapper or generated operation function-call step.

binding_kind.description: Generated call-site kind that reaches the package-domain source symbol.

facade_path.description: Generated runtime facade path containing the wrapper.

operation_path.description: Generated operation contract path containing the exact function-call step.

operation_id.description: Generated operation id containing the exact function-call step.

source_module.description: Package-domain source module imported by the generated facade wrapper.

source_symbol.description: Exact package-domain source symbol imported by the generated facade wrapper.

operation_ids.description: Generated operation ids allowed to reach this source symbol.

primitive_refs.description: Runtime primitive refs allowed to reach this source symbol.

minimization_category.description: Issue #1357 minimization classification used to distinguish true primitive ownership from generated-command behavior that must move.

minimization_route.description: Implementation route for shrinking or retaining this exact runtime boundary.

minimization_owner.description: Reviewable owner for the current boundary and the next minimization decision.

minimization_tracking_issue.description: Issue that owns future extraction, migration, or acceptance of this boundary.

direct_edit_reasons_allowed.description: The only direct runtime-source edit reasons allowed for this boundary.

status.description: Only accepted-permanent-package-domain-boundary can support full Python completion.

stale_when.description: Condition that makes this accepted runtime boundary stale or no longer acceptable.
